title: Refine Metrics Analyst UX — Simplified First-Time Experience
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want a simplified and consistent Metrics Analyst interface with clear Add/Ignore actions and streamlined categorization, so that I can process metric suggestions efficiently without getting lost in complex controls.
description:
Refine the Metrics Analyst results UX to create consistency with the Snowflake workflow and simplify the experience for first-time users.

### UX Changes

**Dashboard Link Icon**
- Replace the existing icon with the Salesforce icon style
- Opens the Salesforce dashboard in a new window
- Tooltip: "Open Salesforce Dashboard"

**Badge/Celebration**
- Move the congratulations badge to appear at the very end of the process on the Metrics Glossary page, not mid-flow
- After categorization, redirect to the Metrics Glossary and display the badge there

**Suggested Metrics Table**
- Replace green/orange highlighted text with a standard data grid using regular text
- Remove checkboxes from the metrics list
- Add individual "Add" and "Ignore" buttons per row instead of bulk checkboxes
- Instruction text: "Add or ignore recommended metrics from your dashboard"
- When all metrics are processed (added or ignored), proceed to categorization step

**Simplified Mode for New Users**
- When the glossary has ≤1 metric, hide the merge functionality and sidebar to keep the experience ultra-simple
- Merge functionality remains available for users with >1 existing metric ("Expert Mode")

**Categorization Step**
- Rename from "Categorized" to "Categorize"
- After categorization, redirect to Metrics Glossary with congratulations badge

**De-duplication**
- Front-end deduplication of metric names returned by the AI API (temporary fix until Metric Analyst V2 service handles this server-side)

### Edge Case: All Metrics Ignored
- If user ignores all suggested metrics and nothing is selected, provide guidance to restart or leave the page
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard link uses Salesforce icon and opens dashboard in a new window with tooltip
Congratulations badge appears on Metrics Glossary page after completing the full flow
Suggested metrics table uses standard data grid without green/orange highlighting
Each metric row has individual Add and Ignore buttons instead of checkboxes
Merge sidebar is hidden when glossary has 1 or fewer metrics
Duplicate metric names are deduplicated on the front end
Completing categorization redirects to Metrics Glossary page
If all metrics are ignored, user receives guidance to proceed
ux_changes: Salesforce icon for dashboard links, standard data grid for suggested metrics, individual Add/Ignore buttons replacing checkboxes, hidden merge sidebar for new users (≤1 metric), renamed 'Categorize' step, badge relocated to Metrics Glossary page after completion, front-end metric deduplication.
implementation_notes: Green/orange text coloring was based on known_metric and known_metric_by_formula API response flags — these conditions were removed for a cleaner table. Front-end deduplication by metric name is a temporary fix until the Metric Analyst V2 service handles server-side deduplication. Demo video shared via Loom for review.
comment_summary: Ryan provided detailed UX feedback with screenshots after reviewing a demo video. Key changes: replace icon, move badge to end, simplify table with Add/Ignore buttons, hide merge for new users, rename categorization step. Waqar confirmed the duplicate metrics come from the API, not the front end, and implemented front-end deduplication. Discussion about handling the case when all metrics are ignored. Waqar redirects to metrics page on completion and asked about badge timing logic.
